feat: Phase 1 — operational pipeline with delta alerting

New src/henri/ package:
- run_all.py: orchestrator with 7 pipeline steps, each in try/except.
  Steps: cleanup → snow_extract → snow_parse → grafana → osint → deltas → reports
- delta.py: compare risk scores across runs, flag ±15 point changes
  as ESCALATION/DE-ESCALATION. Atomic write for risk_scores.json.
- __main__.py: argparse dispatcher for python -m henri [run_all|report]

CLI: python -m henri run_all [--dry|--fixtures|--source X|--field-only]
     python -m henri report [--field-only]

Report changes:
- generate_report() now accepts deltas and output_name params
- Timestamped output: henri_field_YYYY-MM-DD.html, henri_full_YYYY-MM-DD.html
- Section 0 "Significant Changes" with red/green banners for alerts

Data management:
- Raw files >30 days auto-cleaned on each run
- risk_scores.json persisted for cross-run delta comparison

Docker: entrypoint python -m henri, command run_all

294 tests passing (17 new for pipeline, delta, cleanup).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

{"tool_name":"Write","tool_input":{"file_path":"/Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri/Dockerfile","content":"# Pinned base images for reproducibility and supply-chain security
FROM python:3.13-slim@sha256:739e7213785e88c0f702dcdc12c0973afcbd606dbf021a589cab77d6b00b579d AS base
FROM ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest@sha256:c4f5de312ee66d46810635ffc5df34a1973ba753e7241ce3a08ef979ddd7bea5 AS uv

FROM base

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.title="HENRI" \
      org.opencontainers.image.description="Humanitarian Early-warning Network Resilience Intelligence" \
      org.opencontainers.image.vendor="ICRC"

WORKDIR /app

# Install uv from pinned image
COPY --from=uv /uv /usr/local/bin/uv

# Install Python dependencies first (layer caching)
COPY pyproject.toml uv.lock ./
RUN uv sync --frozen --no-dev

# Install Playwright headless chromium + system deps, then clean up
RUN uv run playwright install --with-deps chromium \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/*

# Create non-root user with no login shell
RUN useradd -m -u 1000 -s /usr/sbin/nologin henri \
    && chown -R henri:henri /app

# Copy source
COPY --chown=henri:henri . .

# Drop to non-root
USER henri

# Health check — verify Python can import the package
HEALTHCHECK --interval=300s --timeout=10s --retries=1 \
    CMD ["python", "-c", "import henri"]

# Default entry point
ENTRYPOINT ["uv", "run", "python", "-m", "henri"]
"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_01DWTJRo6e1GtbQWxy4t537M","tool_output":"{"type":"update","filePath":"/Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri/Dockerfile","content":"#   henri:
    build:
      context: .
      network: host
    network_mode: host
    env_file: .env
    volumes:
      - ./data:/app/data
    read_only: true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp:size=100M
    security_opt:

Every Prometheus/Grafana FortiGate target carries it. details:
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri add src/snow_analyse/precursor.py src/henri/forward_alert.py src/snow_analyse/baseline_report.py templates/baseline_report.html.j2 tests/test_precursor.py && git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Phase 2 — temporal precursor analysis and forward-looking alerts

Precursor scanner (src/snow_analyse/precursor.py):
- For each of 218 surge events, looks backwards across OSINT sources
- ACLED: 7-day lookback, flags 2x baseline conflict spikes
- Cloudflare: 24h lookback for outage annotations
- Internal: 24h lookback for WAN latency alerts before site-down
- Result: 47 of 218 surges (22%) had detectable external precursors

Forward alerting (src/henri/forward_alert.py):
- Checks last 48h of ACLED data against 30-day baselines
- 6 countries currently flagged with elevated conflict activity
- Emits warnings with delegation-level at-risk lists
- "Historical pattern suggests network disruption within 24-72h"

Report additions:
- Section 10: Precursor analysis table with lead time color-coding
  (green >48h, yellow 12-48h, red <12h, gray no precursor)
- Summary cards: precursor rate, avg lead time, most reliable source
- Forward alert banners at report top (orange, with delegation list)

307 tests passing (13 new for precursor analysis + forward alerts).

filesAffected:
["feat: Phase 2 — temporal precursor analysis and forward-looking alerts

Precursor scanner (src/snow_analyse/precursor.py):
- For each of 218 surge events, looks backwards across OSINT sources
- ACLED: 7-day lookback, flags 2x baseline conflict spikes
- Cloudflare: 24h lookback for outage annotations
- Internal: 24h lookback for WAN latency alerts before site-down
- Result: 47 of 218 surges (22%) had detectable external precursors

Forward alerting (src/henri/forward_alert.py):
- Checks last 48h of ACLED data against 30-day baselines
- 6 countries currently flagged with elevated conflict activity
- Emits warnings with delegation-level at-risk lists

{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"uv run python -c "
from snow_analyse.precursor import _check_acled_for_countries
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
import pandas as pd

# Create a scenario: baseline 2/day, spike on day -5 with 10 events
surge_start = datetime(2026, 2, 15, 19, 50, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
dates = []
countries = []
# Baseline: 2 events/day for 30 days before window
for d in range(8, 38):
    for _ in range(2):
        dates.append(str((surge_start - timedelta(days=d)).date()))
        countries.append('Syria')
# Normal for days -7 to -6
for d in [7, 6]:
    for _ in range(2):
        dates.append(str((surge_start - timedelta(days=d)).date()))
        countries.append('Syria')
# Spike on day -5: 10 events
for _ in range(10):
    dates.append(str((surge_start - timedelta(days=5)).date()))
    countries.append('Syria')
# Normal for days -4 to -1
for d in [4, 3, 2, 1]:
    for _ in range(2):
        dates.append(str((surge_start - timedelta(days=d)).date()))
        countries.append('Syria')

df = pd.DataFrame({'event_date': dates, 'country': countries, 'fatalities': [0]*len(dates)})
result = _check_acled_for_countries(surge_start, {'SYR': 'Syria'}, df)
print(f'Detected: {result[\"detected\"]}')
print(f'Spike onset: {result[\"spike_onset\"]}')
print(f'Lead time: {result[\"lead_time_hours\"]}h')
print(f'Expected onset: {(surge_start - timedelta(days=5)).date()} = 2026-02-10')
print(f'Expected lead: ~120h (5 days)')
" 2>&1","description":"Verify spike onset detection"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_01PuHkQtB3hDmgR7jne7p8B6","tool_output":"{"stdout":"Detected: False\nSpike onset: None\nLead time: 0h\nExpected onset: 2026-02-10 = 2026-02-10\nExpected lead: ~120h (5 days)","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}","trace_id":"de181ab1-c0e0-479e-b9c7-3f4b649dae1a"}

details:
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri add src/snow_analyse/precursor.py src/snow_analyse/baseline_report.py tests/test_precursor.py && git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: correct precursor lead time calculation and add historical OSINT

Lead time fix:
- Compute daily event counts in the 7-day lookback window
- Find spike onset: first day exceeding mean + 2σ threshold
- Lead time = surge_start - spike_onset (not the window size)
- If spike predates the lookback window, report >168h
- Use threshold = mean + 2*max(std, 0.5*mean) to handle zero-std baselines

Historical OSINT data:
- CF: pull 180-day annotations from live API (47 annotations vs 5 fixture)
- IODA: query /v2/outages/alerts per country for full incident date range
  (4,452 alerts across 51 countries)
- Result: 113 of 218 surges (52%) now have external precursors (up from 22%)

Detection now uses dual gate: daily spike exceeding threshold OR
7-day aggregate ratio >= 2x baseline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri checkout main && git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri merge fix/precursor-lead-time && git -C /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri branch -d fix/precursor-lead-time","description":"Commit, merge, clean up"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_015zJEJk7WEZR2mS18gjQE8d","tool_output":"{"stdout":"[fix/precursor-lead-time 8c8c3a9] fix: correct precursor lead time calculation and add historical OSINT\n 1 file changed, 237 insertions(+), 23 deletions(-)\nSwitched to branch 'main'\nM\tCLAUDE.md\nUpdating 2bcaf54..8c8c3a9\nFast-forward\n src/snow_analyse/precursor.py | 260 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----\n 1 file changed, 237 insertions(+), 23 deletions(-)\nDeleted branch fix/precursor-lead-time (was 8c8c3a9).","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}","trace_id":"de181ab1-c0e0-479e-b9c7-3f4b649dae1a"}

from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from henri.web.app import app

client = TestClient(app)

# Health
r = client.get('/api/health')
print('Health:', r.status_code, r.json())

# Dashboard
r = client.get('/api/v1/dashboard')
print('Dashboard:', r.status_code, 'alerts:', len(r.json().get('alerts',[])), 'risk_cards:', len(r.json().get('risk_cards',[])))

# Countries
r = client.get('/api/v1/countries')
print('Countries:', r.status_code, 'count:', len(r.json().get('countries',[])))

# Countries filtered
r = client.get('/api/v1/countries?tier=high,medium')
print('High+Med:', r.status_code, 'count:', len(r.json().get('countries',[])))

# Country detail
r = client.get('/api/v1/countries/IQ')
print('Iraq:', r.status_code, 'delegations:', r.json().get('country',{}).get('delegations',[])[:3])

# Surges
r = client.get('/api/v1/surges?has_precursor=true&limit=5')
print('Surges:', r.status_code, 'count:', len(r.json().get('surges',[])), 'stats:', r.json().get('stats',{}).get('total_surges'))

feat: Phase 3a — FastAPI backend with dashboard, countries, surges API

New src/henri/web/ package:
- app.py: FastAPI app, CORS, health check, SPA catch-all
- api/router.py: Main router aggregating endpoint modules
- api/dashboard.py: /api/v1/dashboard — risk cards, forward alerts,
  delta alerts, pipeline status in one call
- api/countries.py: /api/v1/countries (list + filter + sort),
  /api/v1/countries/{iso2} (drill-down with ACLED timeline,
  ServiceNow incidents, surge history)
- api/surges.py: /api/v1/surges (precursor table with stats),
  /api/v1/surges/{id} (single surge detail)

Also: save precursor_analysis.parquet during report generation
so the surges API has data to serve.

Security hardening:
- ISO2 validation (alpha-only, 2 chars)
- Region param pattern validation
- Generic 404 messages (no info disclosure)
- Path traversal guard on SPA catch-all
- No credentials in any API response

Dependencies: fastapi>=0.115, uvicorn[standard]>=0.34
15 new tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

uv run python -c "
import ast, sys
# Check db.py for string formatting in SQL
with open('src/henri/web/db.py') as f:
    content = f.read()
# All SQL should use parameterized queries (?)
lines = content.split('\n')
for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
    stripped = line.strip()
    if 'execute' in stripped and ('f\"' in stripped or 'format' in stripped or '%s' in stripped):
        print(f'POTENTIAL SQL INJECTION at db.py:{i}: {stripped}')

# Check scheduler for credential logging

for word in ['password', 'token', 'key', 'secret', 'credential']:
    if word.lower() in sched.lower():
        print(f'WARNING: scheduler.py contains \"{word}\"')
print('Scheduler credential check: CLEAN')

fix(security): rate limiting, file locks, path traversal, error handling

Pipeline DoS prevention:
- 5-minute cooldown between regenerations (rejects with remaining time)
- File-based lock (data/.pipeline.lock) prevents scheduler/API overlap
- Stale locks auto-cleaned after 30 minutes
- Scheduler acquires same lock, skips if held

Path traversal hardening:
- Reject symlinks in report endpoint
- Resolve path before containment check (not after)

Error handling:
- Global exception handler returns generic 500 (no stack traces)
- Removed surge_id from error messages

Added to .gitignore: .pipeline.lock, henri.db

Note: Authentication deferred to OpenShift OAuth proxy deployment.

EOF

{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cd /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri && git add frontend/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: dashboard design system — typography, bento grid, bespoke visualizations

Phase A — Typography & Layout:
- DM Sans (headings/body), DM Mono (data), Noto Sans (footnotes)
- Type scale: display (49px), data-display (39px), heading (20px),
  label (10px uppercase), data (13px mono), small (10px)
- 12-column bento grid dashboard with asymmetric card sizes
- Stat cards: DM Mono 39px numbers with uppercase labels

Phase B — Bespoke SVG Visualizations:
- SurgePulse: EKG-style timeline, cyan spikes for precursor-detected
  surges, gray for non-precursor. Hover tooltips.
- DotHistogram: dot-array incident volume by region × month.
  Region-colored dots stacked bottom-up. Hover highlights.
- SourceHealthRings: concentric SVG arcs showing data source
  freshness. Color-coded by source, dashed for unavailable.
- DotSparkline: 30-dot risk score sparklines in risk table rows.
  Color interpolated green→yellow→orange→red by score.

Phase C — Three.js Mini-Globe:
- Dot-matrix earth: delegation country dots sized and colored by
  risk tier. Wireframe sphere at 8% opacity.
- Slow auto-rotation (~1 rev/60s), pauses on hover.
- Click navigates to /globe. Transparent background.
- Country centroids for 60+ ICRC delegation countries.

TypeScript: zero errors. Build: clean. No axios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: dashboard design revision — 9 fixes for layout, readability, polish

1. Compact alerts: stacked banners → single collapsible summary card
   with country multipliers and delegation pills
2. Dot-matrix map: replaced Three.js globe with dotted-map flat world
   map. Delegation dots colored by risk tier. Removed three.js dep.
3. Risk table: full-width row colors via border-collapse + px-4 padding
4. Stat cards: 4-col each with explanatory one-liners below the numbers
5. Surge pulse: added "last 90 days" label, month axis, precursor legend
6. Source health: added title label and source legend with colored dots
7. Dot histogram: added title, month labels, region color legend, 6px dots
8. Collapsed sidebar: 56px icon-only with tooltips, pipeline status dot
9. Top delegations: filtered to field regions (no HQ/GVA)

Grid: 12-col bento with viewport-filling auto-rows. Map span-5,
risk table span-7, stat cards span-4 each.

EOF

{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cd /Users/pcs14317/Projects/henri && git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: dashboard revision round 2 — masonry, bar chart, status list

1. 3-column masonry grid: cards size to content, no fixed heights.
   Map 1-col, risk table 2-col, stat cards 1-col each.
2. Dashboard title: "HENRI — Network intelligence" + last-refreshed
   timestamp in UTC.
3. Map container: fit-to-content, padding 8px, no height constraints.
4. (Stat cards kept as-is)
5. Surge pulse: EKG spikes → Recharts weekly stacked bar chart.
   Cyan = had precursors, gray = no precursors. Month labels + legend.
6. Source health: concentric rings → clean status list with colored
   dots, metric counts, freshness timestamps ("2min ago").
7. Dot histogram: month labels repositioned below dots with 16px gap,
   common baseline for all months.
8. Alert one-liner: "Conflict activity above normal levels — network
   disruption possible within 24-72h"

details:
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git add src/henri/web/api/dashboard.py frontend/src/components/SourceHealthRings.tsx && git commit -m "feat: dynamic source metrics in health card

Backend computes real record counts from data files:
- ServiceNow: parquet row count (50,000 incidents)
- Grafana: grafana-sourced delegation count (110 sites)
- NetBox: circuit count (64 circuits)
- ACLED: parquet row count (62,397 events)
- IODA: countries with outage scores from risk cards
- Cloudflare: countries with outage events from risk cards

Frontend reads metric from API instead of hardcoded values.

feat: countUp.js odometer animations on all numeric data

Added countup.js for smooth number animations across all pages:

- Dashboard: stat cards (surges/total, detection %, lead time hours)
- Surges: total surges, precursor count, avg lead time
- Country: ACLED events, fatalities, IODA score, SiteDown count
- Delegations: incident count per delegation card

New files:
- hooks/useCountUp.ts: reusable hook wrapping CountUp instance
- components/AnimatedNumber.tsx: drop-in <AnimatedNumber value={n} />
  with decimals, suffix, prefix, and duration props

Numbers animate on first load and re-animate when values change
(e.g. after pipeline regeneration).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv()
import os, time, httpx

token = os.getenv('GRAFANA_API_TOKEN')
url = 'https://grafana.ext.icrc.org'
ds_id = '4'
now = int(time.time())
week_ago = now - 7 * 86400

# Test instant (works in production code)
r = httpx.get(
    f'{url}/api/datasources/proxy/{ds_id}/api/v1/query',
    headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'},
    params={'query': 'up{job=\"fortigate\"}'},
    timeout=15,
)
print(f'Instant: {r.status_code}')

# Test range
r = httpx.get(
    f'{url}/api/datasources/proxy/{ds_id}/api/v1/query_range',
    headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'},
    params={'query': 'up{job=\"fortigate\"}', 'start': str(week_ago), 'end': str(now), 'step': '3600'},
    timeout=15,
    follow_redirects=True,
)
print(f'Range GET: {r.status_code}, history: {[h.status_code for h in r.history]}')
print(f'Data results: {len(r.json().get(\"data\", {}).get(\"result\", []))}')

action:
tool_use: bash — git add .env.example src/henri/web/scheduler.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add scheduler config to .env.examp
details:
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git add .env.example src/henri/web/scheduler.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add scheduler config to .env.example, improve misfire handling

.env.example: added HENRI_SCHEDULER_ENABLED, SCHEDULER_FULL_HOUR,
SCHEDULER_FULL_MINUTE, SCHEDULER_OSINT_INTERVAL_MINUTES.

Scheduler: added misfire_grace_time (1h for full pipeline, 5min for
OSINT quick check) and coalesce=True to handle container restarts
gracefully — if a scheduled run is missed, it fires once when the
container comes back up (within the grace window).

Pipeline run verified: 7/7 steps OK, 183.6s. Delta alerting detected
31 score changes. Grafana live fell back to fixture (intermittent 302).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git checkout main && git merge fix/env-scheduler-config && git branch -d fix/env-scheduler-config","description":"Commit, merge"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_01Kpp5w5DEzJhLSnZPStvM1c","tool_output":"{"stdout":"[fix/env-scheduler-config 6628298] feat: add scheduler config to .env.example, improve misfire handling\n 2 files changed, 10 insertions(+)\nSwitched to branch 'main'\nUpdating fa3408c..6628298\nFast-forward\n .env.example               | 6 ++++++\n src/henri/web/scheduler.py | 4 ++++\n 2 files changed, 10 insertions(+)\nDeleted branch fix/env-scheduler-config (was 6628298).","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}","trace_id":"de181ab1-c0e0-479e-b9c7-3f4b649dae1a"}

{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git add src/grafana_client/client.py src/henri/run_all.py src/henri/web/api/dashboard.py \
  frontend/src/components/SourceHealthRings.tsx frontend/src/styles/globals.css .env.example \
  && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Grafana retry logic + source health fallback indicators

Grafana retry:
- Configurable via GRAFANA_RETRY_ENABLED, GRAFANA_RETRY_WAITING_TIME (180s),
  GRAFANA_RETRY_TIMES (5). All queries go through _request_with_retry.
- On failure: waits, resets httpx client, retries. After all attempts
  exhausted, returns None (caller falls back to fixture).

Source health tracking:
- Pipeline writes source_status.json with "live" | "fallback" per source
- Dashboard API reads it: fallback sources get status="fallback" and
  null last_pull (timestamp not updated)
- Frontend: fallback sources show pulsating orange dot (3s slow-pulse
  animation) with tooltip "Fetching from source failed, reverted to
  previously stored dataset (see logs for detail)"
- Freshness shows "using cache" instead of a stale timestamp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git checkout main && git merge feat/grafana-retry-source-health && git branch -d feat/grafana-retry-source-health","description":"Commit, merge"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_01TG2sLt5GgyD6AZMd3AeSYp","tool_output":"{"stdout":"[feat/grafana-retry-source-health c9616d3] feat: Grafana retry logic + source health fallback indicators\n 6 files changed, 143 insertions(+), 51 deletions(-)\nSwitched to branch 'main'\nUpdating 6628298..c9616d3\nFast-forward\n .env.example                                  |   3 +\n frontend/src/components/SourceHealthRings.tsx |  16 ++-\n frontend/src/styles/globals.css               |   9 ++\n src/grafana_client/client.py                  | 139 +++++++++++++++++---------\n src/henri/run_all.py                          |  20 +++-\n src/henri/web/api/dashboard.py                |   7 +-\n 6 files changed, 143 insertions(+), 51 deletions(-)\nDeleted branch feat/grafana-retry-source-health (was c9616d3).","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}","trace_id":"de181ab1-c0e0-479e-b9c7-3f4b649dae1a"}

fix: switch all Grafana queries to POST, disable re
details:
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git add src/grafana_client/client.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: switch all Grafana queries to POST, disable redirect following

The reverse proxy in front of Grafana intermittently returns 302
for GET requests with long query strings. This is a permanent
infrastructure constraint.

Changes:
- query_instant and query_range now use POST with form-encoded body
  (Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded)
- follow_redirects=False on httpx client — 302 leads to HTML login
  page that breaks JSON parsing
- 302-specific warning: "likely reverse proxy redirect"
- Retry still works: on 302, waits and retries with POST

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git checkout main && git merge fix/grafana-post-method && git branch -d fix/grafana-post-method","description":"Commit, merge"},"session_id":"c53a611e-6873-4ce3-ba35-d170b5c1ed63","tool_use_id":"toolu_01JxywzHtArdCfpMDu9PFwxt","tool_output":"{"stdout":"[fix/grafana-post-method 18dd238] fix: switch all Grafana queries to POST, disable redirect following\n 1 file changed, 11 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)\nSwitched to branch 'main'\nM\tdata/reference/delegations.json\nM\tdata/reference/subsite_map.json\nUpdating c9616d3..18dd238\nFast-forward\n src/grafana_client/client.py | 17 +++++++++++------\n 1 file changed, 11 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)\nDeleted branch fix/grafana-post-method (was 18dd238).","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}","trace_id":"de181ab1-c0e0-479e-b9c7-3f4b649dae1a"}
